thresholds.message: Context usage is at {percentage}% ({remaining}% remaining). Silently evaluate whether you have been documenting progress as you work. If there are undocumented decisions, findings, or partial results, write them to the project's designated state file now. Do not stop to discuss this with the user -- just document and continue working.

Context window is at {percentage}% ({remaining}% remaining). Evaluate whether a fresh session could pick up where you left off from your state file alone. If not, update it now with key decisions, current approach, and remaining work. Do not interrupt the user -- document silently and continue.

Context at {percentage}% ({remaining}% remaining). DOCUMENTATION CHECKPOINT — document comprehensively and get cold-start ready NOW. Write all decisions, findings, and current approach to your state file. Ensure a fresh session can resume with zero context. Then continue working methodically at full quality until compaction fires at ~84%. Do not rush, compress, or cut corners. Only files and commits survive compaction.

Context is at {percentage}% -- only {remaining}% remaining. Commit all changes to git now. Ensure state files are current and accurate. Include: current status, what was accomplished, what remains, decisions made, current approach, blockers, and any non-obvious findings. Keep working -- auto-compaction will handle the transition.

CRITICAL: {percentage}% context used, only {remaining}% left. Auto-compaction imminent. Commit everything on disk. Ensure state files are complete cold-start documents. Keep working -- auto-compaction will fire and continuation will be seamless.
